div1.entryFree: Supplant, to displace, to remove, to make away with: you did s. your brother Prospero, Tp. II, 1, 271. I will s. some of your teeth, III, 2, 56 (Stephano's speech). you three from Milan did s. good Prospero, III, 3, 70. we must s. those rough rug-headed kerns, R2 II, 1, 156. which fear if better reasons can s. H6B III, 1, 37. lest the people . . . s. you for ingratitude, Tit. I, 447.
